introduction: Blockchain is a decentralized technology that enables secure and transparent digital transactions without relying on a central authority. This virtual lab provides interactive experiments and simulations to help students understand blockchain architecture, consensus mechanisms, smart contracts, and decentralized applications.

experiments.name: Differentiating Blockchain Databases from Conventional Databases
Double Spending and Its Prevention in Blockchain
Transaction on an Ethereum wallet using MetaMask
Understand the Syntax and Coding Process of Smart Contracts
Smart contract vulnerabilities (Re-entrance, Arithmetic overflow and underflow, Accessing private data)

UG: Undergraduate students in Computer Science, Blockchain Technology, and related disciplines

objective: To enable students to understand the principles of blockchain technology, experiment with consensus algorithms, develop smart contracts, and analyze blockchain security through interactive simulations.

courseAlignment.description: The syllabi of this lab aligns to the following universities in India.
universities: Sharda University, NCR Delhi – CSE021: Introduction to Blockchain
UPES, Dehradun – Cryptocurrency (CSBL2002), Blockchain Components and Architecture Lab (CSBL3109)
VIT, Vellore – Design and Development of Blockchain Applications
Ajeenkya D Y Patil University, Pune – Blockchain related courses in Computer Science programs
